task_completion.description: Navigate to the Feeds page in Emoncms
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the Emoncms interface with the 'Feeds' section selected or visible.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog or form to create a new feed
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New feed' dialog or form is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the Name, Tag, and Unit for the new feed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input fields in the new feed form show Name as 'Boiler Power', Tag as 'heat', and Unit as 'W'.

task_completion.description: Select the PHPFina engine and 10 seconds interval
task_completion.visual_evidence: The engine dropdown is set to 'PHPFina' and the interval is set to '10s' or '10 seconds'.

task_completion.description: Verify the new feed is successfully created and listed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The feeds list displays the newly created 'Boiler Power' feed with the tag 'heat', unit 'W', engine 'PHPFina', and 10s interval.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with Emoncms
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser navigating the Emoncms UI, not terminal commands like curl or direct database queries.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the feed list
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no browser developer tools open modifying the DOM to artificially show the feed in the list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Emoncms is accessed via http://localhost. PHPFina is a valid feed engine in Emoncms. The interval should be 10 seconds. The environment contains pre-existing seed feeds (e.g., House Power, Solar PV), but 'Boiler Power' is not among them and must be newly created by the agent.
